function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command inside the sandboxed Docker container.

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Edit a file inside the sandboxed container by replacing exactly one occurrence of old_text with new_text. The file must already exist and the path must be under /workspace.

path.description: Absolute path to the file inside the container, must start with /workspace/

old_text.description: The exact text to find. Must appear exactly once in the file.

new_text.description: The text to replace it with.

function.name: spawn_workers
function.description: Run several sub-agents in parallel, each with its own isolated context, to work on independent sub-tasks at the same time. Use this when you have multiple pieces of work that do not depend on each other (e.g. writing tests for several files, exploring different parts of a codebase). Each worker runs the same agent loop and shares the global token budget. Returns a list of result objects with worker_id, task, result text, and error (if any). Do not use this for sequential work or for a single task — call bash or edit_file directly instead.

tasks.description: List of independent task descriptions, one per sub-agent. Each task should be a complete, self-contained instruction the worker can act on without seeing the others.

function.name: add_task
function.description: Add a task or deadline to the user's list. Use whenever they want to remember to do something or mention a deadline.

description.description: What the task is, in a few words.

due_date.description: Optional deadline as YYYY-MM-DD. Convert any natural-language date ('friday', 'tomorrow') to this format yourself using today's date. Omit if there's no deadline.

function.name: list_tasks
function.description: List the user's tasks. Use for questions like 'what's due this week' or 'what do I need to do'. Returns each task's id, text, and due date.

status.description: Which tasks to show. Defaults to pending.

function.name: complete_task
function.description: Mark a task done by its id. If you don't know the id, call list_tasks first.

task_id.description: The id of the task to complete.

function.name: delete_task
function.description: Delete a task by id. Prefer complete_task for finished work; delete only removes something added by mistake.

task_id.description: The id of the task to delete.
